ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a chicken-and-egg English muffin breakfast burger; mapped to the broader 'sandwich food product' class (FOODON_00002473) as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_description: Burger / sandwich — mixed meat and egg filling
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for a chicken-and-egg English muffin breakfast burger. A0FJP (composite sandwich/burger) is used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 ancestor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept maps precisely to this composite breakfast burger product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 200 g, representing a complete assembled breakfast burger consisting of two English muffin halves, one chicken patty, one fried egg, one slice of cheese, and a spread of mayonnaise.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken and egg breakfast burger — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 'Chicken and egg breakfast burger' (cr_id F08020009). All per-100g values are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — English muffin and mixed sandwich analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a chicken-and-egg English muffin breakfast burger. GI estimated at 55 (low, per the standard threshold of GI ≤55 = low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for English muffin (GI ~55, white wheat-based), which constitutes the dominant carbohydrate vehicle in this dish. The chicken patty, egg, cheese, and mayonnaise components contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (55 × 22.73 × 2.0) / 100 ≈ 25.0, rounded to 25.3 g GL per 200 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 436 mg/100 g; a 200 g serving delivers approximately 872 mg sodium, representing 38% of the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg, with insufficient potassium (304 mg/serving) to offset the sodium load.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this item to occasional consumption, request reduced sauce and no added salt, and monitor cumulative daily sodium intake carefully given that a single serving may contribute over one-third of the recommended daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (22.73 g/100 g; ~45 g per 200 g serving) from a refined English muffin base with a low-to-medium GI (~55); protein (13.75 g/100 g) and modest fibre (1.81 g/100 g) provide partial glycaemic buffering.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes may consume this item in controlled portions (up to 200 g), pairing it with a non-starchy vegetable side to lower the overall glycaemic response; a wholegrain English muffin variant is preferred to reduce GI and improve fibre intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat estimated at ~2.5 g/100 g and trace trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g) from processed bun and mayonnaise, combined with high sodium (436 mg/100 g), present a composite cardiovascular risk in regular consumption.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption, choose a grilled chicken patty over a fried one to reduce saturated and trans fat exposure, and request minimal mayonnaise to lower both saturated fat and sodium contribution per serving.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken and Egg Breakfast Burger is a Western-style fast-food breakfast sandwich popular in Singapore, consisting of a seasoned chicken patty, one or more fried eggs, a slice of processed cheese, and mayonnaise assembled between two toasted English muffin halves. The chicken patty is typically pan-fried or grilled, yielding a savoury, slightly crispy exterior with a moist interior. The fried egg adds richness and a soft, yielding texture, while the melted cheese contributes creaminess and mild saltiness. The English muffin provides a slightly chewy, nook-and-cranny crumb that absorbs the egg yolk and mayonnaise. Nutritionally, the burger delivers 219 kcal per 100 g, with a substantial protein content of 13.75 g/100 g making it a relatively high-protein breakfast option. Fat is moderate at 7.9 g/100 g, carbohydrate is 22.73 g/100 g primarily from the English muffin, and sodium is elevated at 436 mg/100 g. The dish is commonly found in school canteens, fast-food outlets, and coffee shops across Singapore and is suitable for breakfast or a light working lunch. It is classified as halal and high-protein. Clinical considerations include elevated sodium relevant to hypertension management, moderate glycaemic load relevant to type 2 diabetes, and trace trans fat and saturated fat relevant to cardiovascular disease risk. Choosing a wholegrain muffin variant and requesting reduced sauce can meaningfully improve the nutritional profile.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08020009) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore dishes. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using the English muffin as the primary carbohydrate vehicle, which is a defensible analogous estimate. Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated from component analysis consistent with HPB data patterns; these are documented as estimates.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this composite dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F08020009 as of 2026-04-16. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in the HPB source. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient data beyond the reported panel are not available in the HPB source for this record. saturated_fat_g (2.5 g/100 g) and trans_fat_g (0.1 g/100 g) are estimated from component analysis of the dish ingredients (chicken patty, fried egg, processed cheese, mayonnaise, English muffin) consistent with HPB data patterns and USDA FoodData Central reference values for analogous components; these are not directly reported in the HPB source. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on the English muffin GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish was found in approved sources. gi_category is set to 'low' as GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low GI range (≤55) per standard University of Sydney GI classification. Although this dish is served in Singapore and local-language names have been provided for completeness and retrieval coverage, it is a Western-style fast-food item; the Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi names are descriptive translations rather than established culinary terms.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3: Added local_names for chinese_simplified ('鸡肉蛋早餐汉堡'), chinese_traditional ('雞肉蛋早餐漢堡'), malay ('Burger Sarapan Ayam dan Telur'), tamil ('சிக்கன் மற்றும் முட்டை காலை உணவு பர்கர்'), and hindi ('चिकन और अंडा नाश्ता बर्गर') to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance.
Iteration 3: Added all local-language name strings to the flat aliases array and to aliases_by_language to satisfy Q8 and the aliases consistency rule.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' to use only allowed values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'], resolving G5 hard gate failure.

Iteration 3: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' as GI value 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low GI range (≤55 = low) per University of Sydney standard classification, resolving Q4 failure.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
